goal: Verify the FailureObserved deduplication path in append_terminal_state_events.py: when the janitor script encounters an error-completed run with no prior FailureObserved, it emits exactly one retroactive FailureObserved. A second invocation of the script on the same events file must NOT emit a duplicate — the first invocation's retroactive event suppresses emission of a second one.
hidden_failure_mode: A change to find_missing_failure_observed() or the retroactive event emission path stops consulting the full event file for existing FailureObserved events, causing duplicate retroactive FailureObserved events to be appended on every script invocation. This silently inflates failure counts in dashboard metrics (state_error_failure_observed_count) and corrupts downstream lifecycle diagnostics without any visible error — the events file just accumulates duplicates. The unit test catches this via the two-invocation pattern; the eval fixture proves the gate still holds across sessions.
